phonetic: 藞t蓴藧t蓹s蕛el; 藞t蓴藧t蓹蕛el
phoneticUs: 藞t蓴藧rt蓹s蕛el藢藞t蓴藧rt蓹蕛el
meaning: adj.榫熺敳鐘剁殑,榫熺敳鑹茬殑
examples: He wears huge spectacles with thick tortoiseshell frames.
It's a Small Tortoiseshell - probably just hatched this morning.
a 14-year-old tortoiseshell cat left behind after its 84-year-old owner, Elue Olvera, died last November.
He paused to interrogate a tortoiseshell kitten that was dozing in a corner of the sofa.
"No, " one staffer in tortoiseshell glasses replied when asked whether she planned to attend a match.
